title: Chunks of Storrow Drive and Soldiers Field Road to be closed Sunday night
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Storrow Drive and Soldiers Field Road in Boston will close from 9 p.m. Sunday to 5 a.m. Monday for tunnel maintenance work.
The closure affects areas between North Harvard Street and Mugar Way, with detours using Eliot Bridge and routes through Cambridge.
Cambridge officials advise caution and note closures could change due to weather, with rain expected Sunday night into Monday.

title: South Boston to crack down on public drinking at St. Pat’s parade, as BPD warns about spiking
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston officials will enforce stricter public drinking laws at the upcoming St. Patrick's Day parade in South Boston.
The Boston Police Department has issued warnings about the risk of drink spiking during the parade weekend.
Local officials collaborate on a task force to address parade safety issues, including public drinking and overcrowding.

title: For $575,000, a historic New Hampshire home with seven laying hens
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: Historic 1812 New Hampshire home in Wolfeboro for sale at $575,000 on 3.3 acres.
The 2,017-square-foot brick home includes three bedrooms, three bathrooms, and a screened-in porch.
Property features a chicken coop with seven hens, which can be rehomed if buyer prefers.

title: A longtime Cape Cod restaurant has closed
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: DiParma Italian Table in West Yarmouth closed after over 20 years, announced by the Kounadis family on social media.
The Kounadis family owns several other restaurants, including Yarmouth House and Tap City Grille, and will continue catering services.
Jacqueline’s on Twenty Eight will replace DiParma, featuring Italian cuisine and an Old Hollywood theme, opening by April.

title: 2 of the most beautiful colleges in the U.S. are in New England, according to Travel + Leisure
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Yale University in New Haven, Conn., ranked No. 15 on Travel + Leisure's list of most beautiful U.S. college campuses.
Wellesley College in Wellesley, Mass., was ranked No. 19 for its scenic 500-acre campus and 19th-century architecture.
Travel + Leisure's list highlights colleges attracting tourists for their aesthetic appeal, including two institutions in New England.

title: Rhode Island stroke survivor relearns to speak practicing his Dunkin’ order
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Rich McMahon from Cranston, Rhode Island, relearns speech using his Dunkin' order after suffering two strokes.
Doctors initially misdiagnosed his stroke as an ocular migraine, leading to a carotid artery dissection.
Local Dunkin' supported his recovery by gifting free coffee and Red Sox tickets after his story went viral.

title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander wins superstar battle vs. Jaylen Brown as Thunder beat Celtics: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander led the Thunder with 35 points in a 104-102 win over the Celtics.
Gilgeous-Alexander broke Wilt Chamberlain's record by scoring 20 points in 127 consecutive games.
Jaylen Brown scored 34 points for the Celtics, who missed Derrick White and Jayson Tatum in the game.

title: Patriots’ riskiest free-agent signing hopes injuries are in the past
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots sign offensive lineman Alijah Vera-Tucker to a three-year, $42 million contract with $17 million guaranteed.
Vera-Tucker missed the entire 2025 season due to a torn triceps, his third major injury in five NFL seasons.
The contract includes $250,000 per-game active bonuses, totaling $4.25 million annually if Vera-Tucker plays every game.

title: NH mom running Boston Marathon for Spaulding after son’s friend injured at beach
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Jessica O'Brien, 38, from Milford, NH, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
O'Brien's motivation comes from Aiden Sloan's recovery at Spaulding after a near-paralysis accident at Hampton Beach.
She is raising funds for Spaulding to support its specialized therapies and compassionate care for patients and families.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a news quiz to test knowledge on trending Greater Boston stories this week.
The quiz is designed to keep readers informed about local news developments and events.
Peter Chianca, Boston.com's general assignment editor, oversees the content, drawing on his extensive experience in the area.
